users.name: Vishal Jaiswar
users.bio: Backend dev who self-hosts everything. If it has an API, I've probably automated it. Currently deep in home-lab rabbit holes.

users.name: Sachin Yadav
users.bio: Tooling nerd building small scripts for small problems. Will happily spend four hours automating a five-minute task and call it a win.

users.name: Mohit Kumar
users.bio: Frontend & design systems. I care far too much about spacing tokens, button states, and getting dark mode exactly right.

users.name: Harshit Tripathi
users.bio: Hardware tinkerer and weekend maker. Pi Picos, 3D prints, and keyboards held together by hope and solder.

posts.content: Turned an old laptop into a home server this weekend. Docker Compose running Pi-hole, a self-hosted wiki, and nightly backups to an external drive. Network-wide ad blocking alone made it worth the afternoon.

comments.text: Pi-hole is a game changer. Add Unbound next to it for recursive DNS so you stop leaking every query to an upstream resolver.
How are you doing backups? I switched to restic on a cron job — encrypted, deduplicated, and restores have actually saved me twice.
Saving this. Been meaning to repurpose my old ThinkPad instead of letting it collect dust on a shelf.

posts.content: Set up a pre-commit hook that runs the formatter and lint --fix before every commit. Took fifteen minutes and I've completely stopped pushing broken formatting. Wish I'd done it years ago.

comments.text: If you want it shareable across the team, the pre-commit framework keeps all the config in one YAML file and works for any language.
Pro tip: pass --cache to the linter so it only checks changed files. Took my hook from ~8s down to under a second.
Just make sure CI runs the same checks too — a hook only protects the people who installed it.

posts.content: Refactored our component library to use CSS custom properties for every color and spacing value. Dark mode is now a single class on the root element instead of a 400-line override sheet. Night and day difference.

comments.text: This is the way. Next step: generate the tokens from one JSON file so design and code share a single source of truth.
Are you honoring prefers-color-scheme so it follows the OS by default? Small touch, but people really notice it.
How did you name the tokens — semantic like --color-surface, or literal like --gray-100? Semantic scaled way better for us.

posts.content: Built a 12-key macro pad over the weekend. Raspberry Pi Pico, hand-wired switches, flashed with QMK. Mapped a whole layer to my most-used editor shortcuts and git commands. Already can't work without it.

comments.text: QMK is so good. Set up a leader key so you can chain commands instead of burning a physical key on every shortcut.
Did you 3D print the case? Would love the STL if you're open to sharing it.

posts.content: Cut our app's load time from 4.2s to 0.9s: route-based code splitting, lazy-loaded images with explicit dimensions, and dropping a heavy date library for the native Intl API. Lighthouse went from 58 to 96.

comments.text: Setting explicit width and height to kill layout shift is so underrated. CLS is the metric everyone forgets until it tanks the score.
What did you use to find the heavy bundles? A bundle visualizer made it painfully obvious where my dead weight was hiding.
Nice numbers. If you want to shave more, preload your fonts and set font-display: swap so text isn't invisible while they load.

posts.content: Wrote one shell script that bootstraps a fresh machine: installs my CLI tools, clones my dotfiles, configures the shell, and sets up git. New laptop to fully working in about ten minutes.

comments.text: Dotfiles plus a bootstrap script is the best gift you can give future-you. I keep mine in a bare git repo so I don't need any symlink juggling.
When you outgrow the script, Ansible does the same thing declaratively and is idempotent — re-running only fixes whatever drifted.
